llm_result.summary: The case shows episodic, sudden anger with near-full-body arousal, frequent trigger sensitivity, surprise at reactions, post-episode shame, identity-discrepancy, some inhibition of outward expression, and a clear motivation to change. Modifiable predictors span bio (arousal regulation, sleep, inhibitory control), psycho (trigger appraisal, expectancy management, self-compassion, values/identity alignment, motivation), and social (support, accountability). The proposed levers aim to reduce episode frequency and intensity, dampen physiological arousal, improve cognitive-emotional regulation, repair self-view after episodes, and boost readiness to engage in skills, with straightforward measurements and practical time horizons.

solutions.solution_candidate: Anger Episode Monitoring with Pause & Plan
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Structured self-monitoring increases awareness of triggers and early cues, enabling timely use of planned coping strategies to prevent or attenuate surprise spikes.
solutions.measurement: Weekly count of anger spikes; time of onset; trigger log; adherence to pause plan (yes/no)
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns anticipated; monitor for avoidance or excessive rumination.
solutions.mapping_query: Map C01 reduction through diary tracking and proactive coping pauses.

solutions.solution_candidate: Autonomic Regulation via Paced Breathing + Regular Aerobic Activity
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Controlled breathing and aerobic activity lower peak physiological arousal during episodes, reducing bodily intensity and downstream intensity of anger.
solutions.measurement: Peak arousal rating during episodes (0-10); resting HR/HRV where available; weekly activity minutes
solutions.safety_notes: Check for cardiovascular symptoms; adjust intensity as needed.
solutions.mapping_query: C02 arousal reduction via autonomic regulation.

solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhancing cognitive reappraisal and gradual exposure to low-severity triggers reduces likelihood that minor events escalate into intense anger.
solutions.measurement: Probability of trigger-provoked anger (0-1); EMA prompts after minor triggers; self-report of reappraisal use
solutions.safety_notes: Progress gradually to avoid avoidance; monitor for overexposure.
solutions.mapping_query: C03 reduce trigger sensitivity through cognitive reappraisal and exposure.

solutions.solution_candidate: Pre-episode Expectancy Management (Mental Rehearsal & Coping Commitments)
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Pre-emptive planning for coping reduces unexpectedness and stabilizes initial emotional responses at episode onset.
solutions.measurement: Subjective unexpectedness rating during episodes (0-1); adherence to planned coping steps
solutions.mapping_query: C04 reduce unexpectedness via planning and rehearsal.

solutions.mechanism_rationale: Developing self-kindness and explicit repair strategies lowers post-episode shame and repetitious self-criticism, breaking the shame cycle.
solutions.measurement: Post-episode shame rating (0-1); frequency of self-criticism; number of repair attempts
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for excessive reassurance seeking or avoidance of self-criticism entirely; refer if rumination worsens.
solutions.mapping_query: C05 shame reduction through self-compassion & repair.

solutions.mechanism_rationale: Clarifying values and aligning actions with an intentional self-view reduces perceived mismatch between anger responses and who one wants to be.
solutions.measurement: Identity alignment score; values clarity index; alignment of last 2-week actions with stated values
solutions.mapping_query: C06 enhance identity-action alignment through values work.

solutions.solution_candidate: Inhibitory Control Training + Safe Expression Outlets
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Strengthening prefrontal control and providing safe outlets increases the ability to pause and avoid outward aggression during episodes.
solutions.measurement: Pause-before-action success rate; self-reported inhibitory control; frequency of verbal/physical expressions without harm
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure outlets are safe and non-harmful; monitor for overuse of outlets as avoidance.
solutions.mapping_query: C07 improve inhibitory control to prevent outward aggression.

solutions.solution_candidate: Motivational Interviewing & Goal-Setting for Anger Management
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Raising motivation and concrete goals increases engagement with skills and persistence through difficult episodes.
solutions.measurement: Readiness-to-change score; session engagement metrics; goal attainment rate
solutions.mapping_query: C08 strengthen motivation to change through MI and goal setting.

solutions.mechanism_rationale: Structured social support reduces trigger reactivity and enhances motivation through accountability and modeling of adaptive responses.
solutions.measurement: Number of supportive contacts per week; adherence to accountability checks; reported trigger responses
solutions.safety_notes: Cultivate supportive networks; avoid unsupervised high-risk groups.
solutions.mapping_query: Social support influence on trigger sensitivity (C03) and motivation (C08).

solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved sleep quality and regular circadian rhythm reduce baseline arousal and improve emotional regulation, dampening reactivity to triggers.
solutions.measurement: Sleep duration/quality (sleep diary or wearable); episode arousal ratings; trigger responsiveness
solutions.safety_notes: Monitor for sleep disturbances or worsening fatigue; refer if sleep disorder suspected.
solutions.mapping_query: Sleep quality effects on arousal (C02) and trigger sensitivity (C03).
